title: Positioning & Brand Differentiation

introduction.subtitle: Positioning maturity assessment. 7-9 min.
introduction.description:
Positioning & Brand Differentiation helps you learn the vocabulary and typical expectations behind ICP & Trigger Clarity, Category & Alternatives, Value & Proof, Message Consistency & Learning. Use it as a private diagnostic: answer from observable evidence, not aspiration, and treat the result as a map for the next improvement cycle.

This educational quiz runs entirely in your browser. You receive the baseline result without account creation or email.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is explicit and share-safe.
introduction.disclaimer: Educational diagnostic only. Not legal, tax, financial, investment, medical, employment, regulatory, or psychometric certification advice.

questions.text: How precisely is the ideal customer profile defined?

options.text: A. The answer is broad, assumed, or based mostly on internal belief.
B. There is a high-level description, but segmentation, trigger, or buyer evidence is thin.
C. The answer is defined for a specific segment with documented behaviour, alternatives, and decision criteria.
D. The answer is validated across repeated cases and directly informs targeting, messaging, prioritisation, and trade-offs.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: ICP & Trigger Clarity: How precisely is the ideal customer profile defined
conceptHelp.summary: ICP & Trigger Clarity means knowing the exact customer profile and the event or pain that makes that customer actively search for a solution now. This question narrows the concept to "How precisely is the ideal customer profile defined?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: ICP & Trigger Clarity matters because founders, investors, partners, and teams need to know whether this area can guide decisions under pressure, not just sound good in a plan.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable evidence: decisions, customer conversations, metrics, owners, records, reviews, policies, deal materials, or shipped work. Choose a high answer only when the evidence has changed a real choice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show how the team answers "How precisely is the ideal customer profile defined?" today, who owns it, what evidence supports it, and one recent decision or trade-off it changed.

questions.text: How clearly is the differentiated category or frame articulated?

options.text: A. This is not explicit yet in Category & Alternatives; decisions depend on intuition, habit, or one-off judgement.
B. There is a plausible practice in Category & Alternatives, but evidence, ownership, or repeatability is still partial.
C. Category & Alternatives has a documented practice with examples, owners, and current evidence used in decisions.
D. Category & Alternatives is reviewed, measured over time, and strong enough to guide trade-offs under pressure.
Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: How strong is the proof behind core claims?

options.text: A. The answer relies mainly on opinion, pitch, memory, or isolated examples.
B. There are supporting examples, but sources are partial, informal, or hard to compare.
C. There is documented evidence from relevant customers, operations, metrics, or records.
D. The evidence is triangulated, current, owner-reviewed, and strong enough to change a material decision.
Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: How well can messaging name the buyer's urgent job and trigger?

options.text: A. No concrete example shows buyer job and demand trigger clarity; the answer relies on intention, memory, or individual judgement.
B. There are isolated examples of buyer job and demand trigger clarity, but they are inconsistent, hard to verify, or depend on one person.
C. Recent examples show buyer job and demand trigger clarity in practice, with enough detail to judge quality and repeatability.
D. Buyer job and demand trigger clarity is repeatedly demonstrated under pressure and changes decisions, ownership, or trade-offs.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: ICP & Trigger Clarity: How well can messaging name the buyer's urgent job and trigger
conceptHelp.summary: ICP & Trigger Clarity is tested here through a concrete operating question: "How well can messaging name the buyer's urgent job and trigger?". Use the answer choices to separate aspiration from current practice.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: ICP & Trigger Clarity matters because weak practice in this area can make the whole result look more mature than the operating system really is.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable behaviour: artifacts, decisions, customer evidence, owners, reviews, or shipped work. Do not score intention as practice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show current evidence, a named owner, and one recent decision changed by this practice.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
